constraints: Ran only read-only Vitest commands from the repo root.
Did not mutate browser/app session state.
Serialized test-cli usage to respect the single-validator guidance.

commands.observation: A narrow worker-entry-point check passed for signed artifact retrieval; the single targeted case returned 200 with application/json while unrelated index.test.ts cases were intentionally skipped by the name filter.

assertions.title: Full pipeline — create study → launch → browser runs → results
vitestCases: convex/e2eCrossAreaIntegration.test.ts > e2e cross-area integration > runs the full study pipeline, records persona-driven differences, and completes the study lifecycle

steps.action: Run the Convex cross-area integration suite from the repo root.
steps.expected: The study should queue, dispatch two runs, receive callback events, settle the runs, and complete the study lifecycle.
steps.observed: The verbose Vitest case passed and asserted queued → running dispatch, two executeRun invocations, success outcomeCounts.success=2, and final study status completed.

observations: After executeRun actions, run summary asserted terminalCount: 2 and outcomeCounts.success: 2.
The same test advanced the study lifecycle through replaying/analyzing and finished with completedAt set.

assertions.title: Persona context influences browser behavior
vitestCases: convex/e2eCrossAreaIntegration.test.ts > e2e cross-area integration > runs the full study pipeline, records persona-driven differences, and completes the study lifecycle

steps.action: Inspect the persona-differentiation assertions in the passing cross-area Convex suite.
steps.expected: Two runs with different axis values should produce measurable differences in run outputs.
steps.observed: The test compared a careful persona (techSavviness 0.1) versus a fast persona (techSavviness 0.9) and asserted different stepCount, frustrationCount, artifactManifestKey suffixes, and selfReport confidence values.

observations: Careful run asserted status success, stepCount 7, frustrationCount 2, artifact key containing careful-manifest.json, selfReport.confidence 0.66.
Fast run asserted status success, stepCount 4, frustrationCount 0, artifact key containing fast-manifest.json, selfReport.confidence 0.97.
The test explicitly required carefulRun.stepCount > fastRun.stepCount and carefulRun.frustrationCount > fastRun.frustrationCount.

assertions.title: Frustration detection triggers early abandonment
vitestCases: apps/browser-executor/src/crossAreaIntegration.test.ts > cross-area integration > abandons early after repeated friction and returns a manifest key plus self-report

steps.action: Run the worker cross-area integration suite.
steps.expected: Repeated friction should cause an early ABANDONED outcome before maxSteps.
steps.observed: The passing test returned finalOutcome ABANDONED, required stepCount < taskSpec.maxSteps, and asserted frustrationCount >= 3.

observations: The worker response body matched ok: true and finalOutcome: ABANDONED.
The same test verified the run abandoned before maxSteps 10 and carried a generated self-report.
The manifest JSON stored for the run contained a milestone_screenshot artifact entry, showing the abandonment path still persisted artifacts.

assertions.title: Artifact manifest persisted and retrievable
vitestCases: apps/browser-executor/src/crossAreaIntegration.test.ts > cross-area integration > abandons early after repeated friction and returns a manifest key plus self-report
apps/browser-executor/src/index.test.ts > browser executor worker entry point > serves stored artifacts for GET /artifacts/:key

steps.action: Confirm manifest persistence in the worker cross-area suite.
steps.expected: The run should write a manifest to ARTIFACTS and return its key.
steps.observed: The passing test asserted artifactManifestKey === runs/run_cross_area/manifest.json and parsed the stored manifest JSON from the mock bucket.

steps.action: Confirm signed artifact retrieval via the worker entry point.
steps.expected: GET /artifacts/:key should return 200 with the stored JSON body when the signature is valid.
steps.observed: The targeted index.test.ts case passed and asserted HTTP 200, content-type application/json, and response text artifact body.

observations: Manifest persistence: uploads.get('runs/run_cross_area/manifest.json') returned JSON matching runId, finalOutcome ABANDONED, stepCount, frustrationCount, and artifacts[].kind = milestone_screenshot.
Retrieval: the signed /artifacts request returned 200 with application/json and the stored artifact body.

assertions.title: Self-report generated and stored
vitestCases: apps/browser-executor/src/crossAreaIntegration.test.ts > cross-area integration > abandons early after repeated friction and returns a manifest key plus self-report
convex/e2eCrossAreaIntegration.test.ts > e2e cross-area integration > runs the full study pipeline, records persona-driven differences, and completes the study lifecycle

steps.action: Check worker-side self-report generation in the abandonment integration test.
steps.expected: The completion callback and response should include a valid SelfReport.
steps.observed: The worker test passed with selfReport.perceivedSuccess false, hardestPart populated, confidence 0.2, and answers keyed to the post-task question.

steps.action: Check Convex-side storage in the full cross-area pipeline test.
steps.expected: Settled run documents should store persona-specific selfReport values.
steps.observed: The careful and fast settled runs both asserted selfReport.perceivedSuccess true and distinct confidence values (0.66 vs 0.97).

observations: Worker completion payload included selfReport with perceivedSuccess false, hardestPart set, confidence 0.2, and answers['Did you complete the task?'] = false.
Convex settled runs stored selfReport objects with perceivedSuccess true and persona-specific confidence values, proving storage beyond the worker callback surface.

assertions.title: Cost controls evaluated on heartbeat
vitestCases: convex/e2eCrossAreaIntegration.test.ts > e2e cross-area integration > evaluates cost controls on heartbeat for the correct study and requests stop when the budget is exceeded

steps.action: Run the targeted Convex heartbeat/cost-control case.
steps.expected: Heartbeat handling should evaluate budget limits for the current study and request stop only for the affected study/run.
steps.observed: The passing test set maxBrowserSecPerStudy to 1, sent a heartbeat for the first run at timestamp 2000, and asserted shouldStop true plus cancellationRequestedAt only on the first run/study while the second run/study stayed untouched.

observations: recordRunHeartbeat returned shouldStop true when the configured browser-seconds budget was exceeded.
firstRun.lastHeartbeatAt became 2000 and firstStudy/firstRun both received cancellationRequestedAt, while the second study boundary remained unaffected.

assertions.title: Run cancellation respected during execution
vitestCases: convex/e2eCrossAreaIntegration.test.ts > e2e cross-area integration > returns shouldStop when cancellation has already been requested on the run
apps/browser-executor/src/progressReporter.heartbeatAck.test.ts > progressReporter heartbeat acknowledgements > returns shouldStop=true when the callback acknowledges cancellation
apps/browser-executor/src/executeRunHeartbeatCancellation.test.ts > execute-run heartbeat cancellation > terminates the run with ABANDONED when a heartbeat ack returns shouldStop=true

steps.action: Verify the Convex heartbeat ack when cancellationRequestedAt is already set.
steps.observed: The Convex test patched cancellationRequestedAt onto the run, then asserted recordRunHeartbeat returned shouldStop true and updated lastHeartbeatAt to 2000.

steps.action: Verify the worker consumes the heartbeat acknowledgement.
steps.expected: The worker should stop gracefully instead of selecting another action.
steps.observed: The worker cancellation test passed with finalOutcome ABANDONED, stepCount 0, selectAction never called, and a completion update plus cancel milestone rationale 'Run cancelled via heartbeat stop signal'.

observations: Heartbeat ack parsing: progressReporter.sendHeartbeat resolved true when callback JSON returned shouldStop true.
Worker handling: execute-run returned finalOutcome ABANDONED with stepCount 0 and emitted a cancel milestone instead of continuing execution.
Convex handling: a run with pre-set cancellationRequestedAt yielded shouldStop true on the next heartbeat.

frictions.description: The `tuistory` binary was not available on PATH even though the CLI guidance assumes it is installed.
frictions.resolution: Used `bunx tuistory` for the same single terminal session, then captured the required snapshots and screenshots successfully.

summary: Tested 7 assigned cross-area CLI assertions. All passed: the Convex suite covered full-pipeline settlement, persona differences, cost-control heartbeats, and cancellation acknowledgements; the worker suites covered frustration-triggered abandonment, manifest persistence/retrieval, self-report propagation, and graceful shouldStop cancellation handling.
